observations.observation: 7-day GMV jumped from $27,565 to $29,007 (+5.2%) and 7-day receipt count from 536 to 572 (+6.7%) in a single snapshot, the strongest single-period intake surge in the recent signal window. Token acceleration holds at 1.03x, confirming the deceleration event two snapshots ago was transient.

7 new CBSAs appeared this snapshot (16860, 12420, 31080, non-metro US-FL, 35840, non-metro US-GA, 33100), bringing active metros to 24. Non-metro Florida and Georgia entries mark the first Deep South non-metro penetration, extending the geographic arc beyond the Northeast corridor.

Token acceleration holds at 1.035x while velocity compressed further to 0.654x — the divergence between growth rate and circulation speed is widening. 7-day GMV grew modestly from $27,498 to $27,565 on 3 new receipts (533→536), confirming sustained but sluggish intake pace.

8 new CBSAs appeared this snapshot (22220, 36837, 17420, non-metro US-ME, 34700, 15380, 12060, 28580), bringing active metros to 17 in the signal window — 47.1% new this cycle. Starbucks entered the top brand list simultaneously, suggesting physical retail expansion is driving the new geographic entries.

Token velocity sits at 0.632x while acceleration holds at 1.004x — velocity compression is deepening even as growth continues. 7-day GMV climbed to $27,498 and receipt count to 533, confirming sustained intake while circulation remains sluggish.

2 new CBSAs appeared this snapshot (non-metro US-MA, CBSA 14460), bringing active metros to 15 in the signal window. Non-metro Massachusetts is the first New England non-metro entry, extending the Northeast non-metro arc that began with Maine and Maryland.

Token velocity dropped to 0.63x while acceleration holds at 1.004x — the velocity compression is deepening even as growth continues. 7-day GMV climbed from $26,609 to $27,028 and receipt count from 523 to 531, confirming intake is sustained but circulation remains sluggish.

8 new CBSAs appeared this snapshot (non-metro US-NV, 25775, 19820, 37980, 36837, 15380, 12420, 35840), bringing active metros to 22. The diffusion arc continues its non-metro push, now spanning Nevada, Pennsylvania, New York, and multiple unnamed secondary markets simultaneously.

Token acceleration recovered to 1.021x this snapshot after the sharp drop to 0.856x last cycle, while 7-day GMV climbed from $26,227 to $26,609 and receipt count ticked from 519 to 523. The deceleration event appears to have been a single-snapshot correction rather than a sustained reversal.

A new "retail" category appeared this snapshot alongside CBSA 17140 entering the active metro set. This is the first time retail has appeared as a distinct category, suggesting the dataset is capturing a new commercial segment beyond the prior SaaS/grocery/subscription mix.
